rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Validate that AGREE property associations and bindings reference valid AADL features or declared helpers.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match AADL conventions (int, real, bool) to avoid resolution errors.
generic_rules: When resolving AGREE diagnostics, prioritize fixing root causes like missing references or invalid types before adding new architecture.
AGREE contracts must only reference identifiers that are either declared in the same annex or visible in the current AADL scope.
AGREE type declarations must align with AADL scalar type conventions to prevent resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within AADL component types or implementations.
Property associations in AGREE must reference valid AADL features or declared helpers with matching types.
AGREE scalar type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined types.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new identifiers or types in AGREE contracts that are not declared or visible in the AADL model.
Do not add architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, correct the root cause of the unresolved reference.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement and component type vs implementation scopes
